summary:
Helmet cells are pathognomonic for microangiopathic hemolytic anemia due to mechanical fragility from vessel wall damage under high shear forces. They are most classically associated with hemolytic uremic syndrome (HUS), where endothelial injury leads to intravascular hemolysis and the development of these distinctive cellular features.  

(Note: Other causes such as disseminated intracranial coagulopathy or thrombotic thrombic purpura also present similarly but HUS remains the primary condition referenced.) 

---
